events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need respond exactly one valid JSON object matching schema, no prose outside JSON. Need assess situation. Scenario: residential job, concrete & masonry, flashing & weeps, observed flashing not lapped/sealed, rough-complete. Need identify component/condition and defect cues. Decision fail because non-compliant. Risk? Flashing not lapped/sealed can lead water intrusion, medium/high. Residential masonry wall, weeps. Since no immediate life safety, but water damage risk high over time. Maybe high. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 80-90? value 0. sound_source etc defaults empty/0. Findings: flashing lacks laps/seals; improper flashing termination/lap; potential water intrusion. Actions: remove/rework flashing as required, install lapped flashing with sealed laps per manufacturer/TMS 402, install weeps. References: TMS 402, BIA Tech Notes. Need ensure JSON shape. The component_conditions enum allowed includes installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. We can include both installed-defective and non-compliant. order empty list. workable empty list. event_time 0, rate 0, sound_source "". Confidence high ~0.95.

events.label: response
events.text: {"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "rough-complete", "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"], "percent_complete": 80, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.95, "event_time": 0.0, "rate": 0.0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Flashing is installed without required laps and seals.", "Discontinuous flashing creates a path for water intrusion behind the masonry veneer.", "Weeps cannot reliably drain water if flashing is not lapped/sealed to the drainage plane."], "actions": ["Remove or rework the flashing to provide proper laps and sealed joints per manufacturer and TMS 402.", "Ensure flashing extends to the exterior face of the wall and terminates with weeps at the base of the wythe.", "Re-inspect before closing up the wall."], "rationale": "Masonry flashing must be continuous, lapped, and sealed to direct water out through weeps. Flashing that is not lapped or sealed is a defective installation that will leak, violating TMS 402 and BIA guidance.", "references": ["TMS 402", "BIA Tech Notes"]}
